title: Earnings: As of March 31, 2026
content:
The credit union reported net income of $135K as of March 31, 2026. This figure reflects the continued strength of our core lending and investment activities. Interest income totaled $719K, while interest expense amounted to $256K, generating spread income that softened to a lower level compared with prior periods due to market conditions. These results underscore the durability of our primary earnings engine even as external factors influence yield dynamics.

Non-interest income totaled $38K and continues to provide a modest but reliable complement to core earnings. The majority of this income is generated from other non-interest sources ($6K), with additional contributions from fee income ($32K). These revenue streams support diversification, ensuring that our financial model remains resilient across varying economic environments.

Non-interest expense totaled $339K, reflecting continued discipline in managing operating costs. The largest component is professional and outside services ($191K), followed by office occupancy expense ($66K) and travel and conference expenses ($82K). These cost levels are consistent with the scale of services we provide our membership and aligned with our investment in member service quality.

Overall Position:
The credit union continues to generate stable earnings supported by interest income. Non-interest income plays a supportive role, while expense levels remain managed as necessary investments in operations rather than burdens. Sustaining performance will depend on maintaining disciplined cost management and leveraging the strength of our lending portfolio. We are confident that these fundamentals support our long-term financial stability.
